raw IPs, so it doesn't fit the masternode model where each operator
runs a node on their own IP.

  subsequent connections (same model as SSH)
- Add public RPC infrastructure note for future TIME Coin-operated
  nodes that would use Let's Encrypt with real domains

commit.message:
fix: allow mcp.sentry.dev in SDK Skill Drift Detector firewall (#43)

* Initial plan

* fix: add mcp.sentry.dev to allowed domains in SDK Skill Drift Detector workflow

The workflow was failing because the project's .mcp.json configures a `sentry`
MCP server at https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp. When Claude Code runs in the agentic
workflow, it reads the project-level .mcp.json and tries to connect to this
server. Since mcp.sentry.dev was not in the firewall's allowed domains list,
the connection was blocked and Claude Code reported:

  ERR_API: MCP server(s) failed to launch: sentry

The "Parse agent logs for step summary" step detected this error and marked the
job as failed — even though the actual work (creating skill drift issues) was
completed successfully.

Fix: Add mcp.sentry.dev to the allowed domains in both the workflow source file
(skill-drift-check.md) and the compiled lock file (skill-drift-check.lock.yml).

commit.message:
Fix dup cascade and EmitBranchCompare transpiler bugs (#155)

* Fix dup cascade and EmitBranchCompare transpiler bugs

Fix two transpiler bugs that caused the climber sample to produce broken
output with garbled sprites and game-stuck behavior:

1. EmitBranchCompare AbsoluteX optimization: When ldelem_u1 loads a value
   via LDA array,X followed by dup+ldc+bne, the optimizer incorrectly
   converted LDA to CMP (comparing against itself). Fix checks instruction
   history to determine if LDA array,X IS the comparison value (not a
   second operand), and emits CMP #constant instead.

2. Dup cascade save/reload: In cascading if-else using dup+ldc+bne/beq
   pattern, after an if-block executes (with JSR calls that modify A),
   A no longer holds the original value for subsequent else-if checks.
   Fix saves A to TEMP_HI after CMP in branch handlers (STA does not
   affect flags on 6502), and reloads it at subsequent dup instructions.
   Uses IsDupCascadeStart() lookahead to distinguish equality cascades
   from button bitmask patterns (dup+ldc+and+brfalse).

Adds climber sample to TranspilerTests.Write with verified snapshot,
and updates Write test to include fami assembly files for extern method
resolution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: sort fami assembly files for deterministic cross-platform order

Directory.GetFiles returns files in platform-dependent order, causing
the climber test to produce different ROM output on macOS vs Windows.
Sort the file paths to ensure consistent ordering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: fix comment, reset cascade flag, scope fami loading

- Update comment to reference TEMP_HI instead of DUP_TEMP for consistency
- Reset _dupCascadeActive in Pop handler so later unrelated dup sequences
  don't incorrectly trigger cascade save/reload
- Scope fami assembly file loading to only samples that use extern methods
  (climber, fami) to reduce unnecessary IO in the test suite

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update climber.gif screenshot to reflect transpiler fixes

Captures the current dotnes climber output showing correctly rendered
platforms, ladders, and 4 visible sprites. The game level renders
properly after the dup cascade and EmitBranchCompare fixes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stelem_i1 ushort high byte extraction in constant-index path

HandleStelemI1's constant-index path was blindly using the last ldc value
as the store value, ignoring arithmetic operations. For the pattern
actor_yy_hi[0] = (byte)(floor_yy >> 8), the shift count 8 was being
stored instead of the actual high byte of the ushort local.

Added targeted detection for ushort high/low byte extraction patterns:
- (byte)(ushort_local >> 8): emit LDA addr+1 (high byte)
- (byte)ushort_local: emit LDA addr (low byte)
Other arithmetic patterns (like scoreboard's BCD digit extraction) are
left unchanged to avoid regressions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix EmitOamSprDecsp4: depth-tracked compound expression parsing

Rewrote the backward argument scan in EmitOamSprDecsp4 to use stack-depth
tracking instead of the previous flat IsConsumedByAdd approach. This correctly
handles compound oam_spr arguments like (byte)(0x30 + (score >> 4)) and
(byte)(0x30 + (score & 0x0f)) that contain Shr/And/Or/Xor binary operators.

Key changes:
- Binary ops at depth 0 enter compound mode (depth=2); nested ops add depth+1
- Value producers (ldc/ldloc) at depth>0 decrement depth (consumed by binop)
- Value producers at depth==0 are standalone arguments
- Added Ldsfld support for static field args (e.g., oam_off)
- Compound args emit inline: LDA local, [binop], CLC, ADC #const
- Moved music BSS variables to avoid overlap with LocalStackBase

This fixes the infinite loop in oam_clear caused by OAM_OFF=6 (not a
multiple of 4), which was caused by the backward scan misinterpreting
shift/mask operands as standalone oam_spr arguments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stelem_i1 ushort high byte extraction for variable-index path

The variable-index path in HandleStelemI1 was missing Shr/Shr_un handling,
so actor_yy_hi[next_actor] = (byte)(floor_yy >> 8) stored the LOW byte
instead of the high byte. This caused actors 1-7 to have yy_hi values
matching their yy_lo values, making them appear at impossibly high world
Y positions and fail the on-screen check.

Added hasShr/shrValue tracking and ushort high byte extraction to the
variable-index code path, matching the fix already in the constant-index
path. Also handles general right shifts via LSR.

Result: all 18 sprites now render (4 actors x 4 sprites + 2 score digits).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: scope dup cascade save, add compound Mul/Sub emission

- Introduced _dupPendingSave flag so STA TEMP_HI only fires on the branch
  immediately following a dup+ldc compare, not on unrelated bne/beq inside
  the if-body. The dup handler sets the flag, the branch handler consumes it.
- Added Mul (via ASL shifts) and Sub (via SEC/SBC) to compound oam_spr
  argument emission. Added explicit throw for unsupported Div/Rem ops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
iccanalyzer-lite: reorganize validation/runtime safety symmetry

Architecture change: H136 (ResponseCurve measurement count) moved from
library-gated phase to always-run phase. Raw-byte checks must not depend
on library load success — the most dangerous profiles are too malformed
for the library to parse.

Symmetry fixes:
- H47 (always-run raw post): Added nDevCoords>15 check with CFL-076
  annotation and nColors>10000 Describe() DoS check with CFL-078
  annotation. Previously only flagged nDevCoords>100.
- H64 (library phase): Added nColors>10000 Describe() iteration risk
  warning with CFL-078 pattern annotation. Added runtime/validation
  asymmetry documentation in comments.
- H67 (library phase): Added GetNumResponseCurveTypes() > 100 check
  for measurement type count.
- H136: Moved to always-run phase in IccAnalyzerSecurity.cpp
  orchestrator (line 686, after RunRawPostLibraryHeuristics).

New test profiles (3):
- named_color2_large_nsize.icc: ncl2 with 70000 entries, 3 device coords
- xyz_large_array.icc: XYZ tag with 5000 entries (60KB)
- calculator_deep_nesting.icc: MPE with 20 sub-element calculator

New unit tests (17 new, 172 total):
- 7 symmetry tests: H47 raw nDevCoords>15, CFL-076 pattern, nColors>10K,
  CFL-078 pattern, H136 always-runs, XYZ/calc no-hang
- 6 runtime safety tests: all CWE-400 profiles complete without hanging
- 1 real PoC runtime test: timeout-0bec9575 completes analysis
- 3 profile synthesis tests (auto-discovered by extended coverage)

All 172/172 tests passing, 0 ASAN/UBSAN errors.

commit.message:
feat: per-database reconciliation locking with PostgreSQL advisory locks (#19)

* Initial plan

* feat: per-database reconciliation locking and PostgreSQL advisory locks (#6)

Add two layers of serialization to prevent concurrent reconcile operations
against the same database target:

1. In-process lock (tokio Mutex in OperatorContext) for same-replica safety
2. PostgreSQL advisory lock (pg_try_advisory_lock) for cross-replica safety

Lock contention results in controlled requeue with jitter (10-30s) rather
than hard failure. All lock acquisition/release events are observable via
structured tracing logs.

Adds 14 new unit tests covering:
- In-memory lock acquisition, contention, release, and concurrent access
- Advisory lock key derivation (deterministic, collision-resistant)
- Lock contention error variant and display
- Requeue jitter behavior
- Contention delay constant validation

Co-authored-by: hardbyte <855189+hardbyte@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(system): Task Manager, ILM, and Templates troubleshooting pages (#1938)

Adds three read-only troubleshooting pages under System nav for
high-friction operational workflows that previously required raw API
calls.

### New pages

- **Task Manager** (`/cluster-tasks`) — KPI cards (running, cancellable,
long-running >60s, oldest), searchable/sortable table by
action/node/runtime, detail flyover with raw JSON
- **Index Lifecycle Management** (`/ilm`) — KPI cards (managed indices,
error count, policy count, phase distribution), dual-tab
indices/policies tables with error-first default sort, detail flyover
surfacing `failed_step` + `step_info.reason`
- **Index Templates** (`/templates`) — KPI cards (index/component
counts, data-stream enabled, high-priority ≥500), dual-tab
index/component template tables, detail flyover showing composition
order and simulated output

### Implementation

- **Types**: `taskTypes.ts`, `ilmTypes.ts`, `templateTypes.ts` —
flattened row types for table rendering alongside raw API response
shapes
- **Client methods**: `listTasks`, `cancelTask`, `getIlmPolicies`,
`getIlmExplain`, `getIndexTemplates`, `getComponentTemplates`,
`simulateIndexTemplate`
- **Hooks**: `useTasks`, `useIlm`, `useTemplates`,
`useSimulatedIndexTemplate` — standard `useQuery` + `DataFetchResult`
pattern; `useIlm` and `useTemplates` use `Promise.all` for parallel
fetches
- **Manifest**: Tasks promoted from hidden to sidebar-visible; ILM
(order 31) and Templates (order 22) added under System
- **Ingest Pipelines**: pipeline detail panel now includes expandable
processor hotspots with matched processor config details
- **Mocks**: Full mock data + route handlers in
`elasticsearch-mocks.mjs` for all new endpoints
- **Tests**: 39 unit tests (sorting, filtering, metric derivation), E2E
smoke test for route/render, AppSidebar test updated

All pages follow existing conventions: `LinearProgress` (not
`CircularProgress`), `COMPONENT_HEIGHTS` tokens, right-side `Drawer`
flyover, `EmptyState` for zero results, `PageHeader` +
`OverviewInfoCard` layout.

commit.message:
feat: implement Phase 2 — veu-auth handshake package

Add the veu-auth Swift Package implementing the core Emerald Handshake
trust primitive for the Veu protocol (Phase 2 of the POC roadmap).

Modules:
- DeadLink: Ephemeral invitation URI generation/parsing with Ed25519
  signing, Base64URL encoding, expiry validation, and one-time burn
- EmeraldHandshake: X25519 ECDH key exchange, HKDF-SHA-256 Circle key
  derivation, 7-phase state machine (IDLE→CONFIRMED + failure states)
- ShortCode: SAS 8-digit hex code + Aura color derivation via HMAC-SHA-256
- HandshakeSession: High-level orchestrator coordinating the full
  initiate→respond→verify→confirm flow with timeout handling
- Ledger: SQLite3-backed local artifact ledger bootstrapping LEDGER.sql
  schema with Circle/artifact CRUD and burn/purge operations
- VeuAuthError: Domain error enum (11 cases)

Tests: 61 tests across 5 test suites — all passing.
Dependencies: VeuCrypto (local), swift-crypto (Linux), SQLite3 (system).

commit.message:
fix: correct three mobile navbar regressions in App.css

1. Add missing --text-secondary CSS variable to :root
   - mobile-nav-section-title and other elements used var(--text-secondary)
     which was undefined; text rendered invisible/empty on mobile drawer

2. Move mobile drawer CSS outside @media (max-width: 768px) block
   - .mobile-drawer, .mobile-drawer-overlay, and all related classes were
     scoped to the mobile media query; on desktop (>=769px) these classes
     had no styles, so the drawer elements rendered as unstyled blocks in
     the document flow (visible layout corruption on desktop)
   - Base styles (position:fixed, transform:translateX(-100%), z-index) must
     always apply so the drawer stays off-screen until JS opens it

3. Add !important to .search-page-container aside { display: none }
   - The <aside> has an inline style prop with display:'flex'; CSS class
     selectors cannot override inline styles without !important
   - Without this, the 280px sidebar never hides on mobile viewports,
     leaving only ~95px for search columns on a 375px screen

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
cfl: add reference patches CFL-077 through CFL-081 (CWE-400 upstream patterns)

Five reference patches for systemic CWE-400 vulnerabilities in DemoIccMAX.
These are NOT applied to fuzzer builds — fuzzers should continue finding
these patterns. The patches document proposed upstream fixes:

CFL-077: ResponseCurveStruct nMeasurements cap (100K per channel)
CFL-078: NamedColor2 Describe() iteration cap (10K entries)
CFL-079: ApplySequence() runtime depth limit (depth 16)
CFL-080: XYZ/Chromaticity/ColorantTable Describe() output cap (1MB)
CFL-081: DescribeSequence() recursion depth limit (depth 32)

commit.message:
feat: add --insecure and --open-dashboard flags

Add two opt-in security relaxation flags for the dashboard:

--insecure: Sets tunnel to anonymous access so no Azure AD login
is required to reach the tunnel URL. Warns at CLI and in server logs.

--open-dashboard: Disables the dashboard passcode (?key= parameter)
requirement for remote access. Warns loudly at both CLI and startup.

Both flags can also be set via config (tunnel_access: "anonymous",
require_key: false) for persistent configuration. Both default to
the secure behavior — users must explicitly opt in.

Environment variables OTTO_TUNNEL_ACCESS and OTTO_OPEN_DASHBOARD
propagate these settings through the daemon fork.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
network: Enforce TLS by default for P2P and RPC connections

- TLS initialized unconditionally at startup (no longer behind enable_tls flag)
- P2P server and client use TLS unless tls=0 explicitly set in time.conf
- RPC server uses TLS unless rpctls=0 explicitly set (default changed to true)
- time-cli negotiates TLS when rpctls=1 or auto-detected; falls back to HTTP with warning
- Failed TLS handshakes drop the connection (no plaintext fallback)
- Clear startup log: TLS enabled/disabled status
- Added tls=0 parsing in time.conf for operator opt-out (debugging only)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
